steps.title: X-intercepts come from setting the factored form to zero
steps.content: X-intercepts are points where $f(x) = 0$. Since the function is already factored, each factor can be set equal to zero to find all the zeros.

steps.title: Solve each factor for x
steps.content:
Setting each of the three factors equal to zero:
$$x + 5 = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = -5$$
$$x - 2 = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = 2$$
$$3x - 4 = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = \tfrac{4}{3}$$
The x-intercepts are $(-5, 0)$, $(2, 0)$, and $\left(\tfrac{4}{3}, 0\right)$.

steps.title: Identify the choice that is NOT an x-intercept
steps.content: Comparing our three zeros with the answer choices: the remaining alternative gives $\left(-\tfrac{3}{4}, 0\right)$. This value does not match any of our zeros ($-5$, $2$, or $\tfrac{4}{3}$), so it is not an x-intercept. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
